description_long: This object is a tall, slender wildflower with multiple stems branching from a central base. The stems are green and slightly angular, supporting several purple, daisy-like flower heads with numerous thin, radial petals. The leaves are dark green, deeply lobed, and lanceolate with serrated edges, growing alternately along the stems. The flower heads are positioned at varying heights, giving the plant a natural, slightly asymmetrical appearance. The petals are densely packed and radiate outward, creating a soft, feathery look. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, roadside verges, or naturalized green spaces.
description: A tall, green-stemmed wildflower with purple, daisy-like blooms and deeply lobed, serrated leaves. The flower heads are feathery and arranged at different heights along the stems.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the plant, with five prominent purple flower heads and deeply lobed leaves spreading outward.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the leftward spread of the lower leaves and the staggered heights of the flower heads.
description_view_2: Rear view with the stems and leaves more visible, and the flower heads appearing more clustered together.
description_view_3: Right-side view emphasizing the angular arrangement of the stems and the side profile of the flower heads.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, radial configuration, forming a circular, daisy-like head. The petals overlap slightly and are evenly spaced, creating a symmetrical, whorled appearance typical of composite flowers.
